stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of noise level using a noise meter. The measurements have a mean of 99.4 dB and a standard deviation of 11.3 dB. However, calibration tests reveal that a noise meter systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.1% and 7.2%. Estimate the true noise level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 99.4 dB. After correcting for the 5.7% bias: ≈ 105.4 dB. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.1%-7.2%) and sampling variability: [99.1, 111.6]. Point estimate: 105.4 dB.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
